zh.Deddington, Oxfordshire, England: 英國英格蘭奧可福爾迪丁頓
zh.Ludlow, Shropshire, England: 英國英格蘭史洛普爾盧德洛福
zh.lifeSummary: Lady Alice Cornes Mardox是Mary Thouthbeck的女兒，於1480年出生在英國素福爾可惠福。她和Sir Robert Pope至少有3個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1576年11月13日在英國英格蘭史洛普爾盧德洛福享年96歲。

zh-hans.Ludlow, Shropshire, England: 英国英格兰史洛普爾盧德洛福
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Alice Cornes Mardox是Mary Thouthbeck的女儿，于1480年出生在英国素福爾可惠福。她和Sir Robert Pope至少有3个儿子和2个女儿。她去世于1576年11月13日在英国英格兰史洛普爾盧德洛福享年96岁。

nameMeanings.name: Cornes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 English: variant of Corn , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Cornish
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name for someone from the county of Cornwall, from Middle English Cornish, Cornysh formed on the analogy of English, which was usually Normanized as Corneys. Not surprisingly, the surname is common in adjacent Devon, but it is also established as far afield as Suffolk and London.

variants.name: Corn
meanings: English: nickname from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cran ‘crane’ (see Crane ).
English: metonymic occupational name for a maker or user of hand mills, Old English cweorn.
Americanized form of German, Dutch, Czech, or Jewish   Korn  , or a shortened form of any of the composite names formed with this element.

variants.name: Corns
meanings: English (Staffordshire and Lancashire): variant of Cornish or Corn , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Mardox
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Devon): habitational name from Mardon in Hennock (Devon). The placename probably derives from Old English (ge)mǣre ‘boundary’ + dūn ‘hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Alice Cornes Mardox was born in 1480, in Wilford, Suffolk, England as the daughter of Mary Thouthbeck. She had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Sir Robert Pope. She died on 13 November 1576, in Ludlow, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 96.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Alice Cornes Mardox est née en 1480, à Wilford, Suffolk, Angleterre, fille de Mary Thouthbeck. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles avec Sir Robert Pope. Elle est décédée le 13 novembre 1576, à Ludlow, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 96 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Alice Cornes Mardox wurde 1480 in Wilford, Suffolk, England, als Tochter von Mary Thouthbeck geboren. Sie hatte mit Sir Robert Pope mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 13. November 1576 im Alter von 96 Jahren in Ludlow, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Alice Cornes Mardox è nata il nel 1480 nel Wilford, Suffolk, England come figlia di Mary Thouthbeck. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie con Sir Robert Pope. Morì il 13 novembre 1576, nel Ludlow, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 96 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Alice Cornes Mardoxは1480年にイギリススフォルクウィルフォードでMary Thouthbeckの娘として生まれた。彼女はSir Robert Popeとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1576年11月13日にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアルドロウで96歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Alice Cornes Mardox(은)는 1480년 영국 수폴크 윌퍼드에서 Mary Thouthbeck의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Sir Robert Pope(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1576년 11월 13일 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 루들로에서 96세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lady Alice Cornes Mardox nasceu em 1480, em Wilford, Suffolk, Inglaterra filha de Mary Thouthbeck. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas com Sir Robert Pope. Ela faleceu em 13 de novembro de 1576, em Ludlow, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 96 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Alice Cornes Mardox родилась в 1480 г. в местности уилфорд, суффолк, Англия. Её мать звали Mary Thouthbeck. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери от Sir Robert Pope. Она умерла 13 ноября 1576 г. в местности лудлоу, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 96 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Lady Alice Cornes Mardox nació en 1480, en Wilford, Suffolk, Inglaterra como hija de Mary Thouthbeck. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas con Sir Robert Pope. Murió el 13 de noviembre de 1576, en Ludlow, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 96 años.
